Oxydendrum arboreum, commonly known as the sorrel tree or sourwood, is a deciduous tree native to the southeastern United States.

The health benefits of Oxydendrum arboreum include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as asthma. The plant has also been traditionally used to treat digestive issues due to its antiseptic properties.

The bioactive constituents of Oxydendrum arboreum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Oxydendrum arboreum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory and digestive issues.
